It is temple No.73 of the {{ill|lt=New Shikoku Mandala Sacred Sites|Q11501519|WD=Q11501519|1=Shin-Shikoku Mandara Pilgrimage}}. === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|lt=Ōgetsuhime|Q3261482|WD=Q3261482|lt=Ōgetsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=Ōgetsuhime}} (おおげつひめのみこと) - also called Ame-no-Iwato-wake Yakura-hime-no-Mikoto or Ōawa-hime-no-Mikoto. According to shrine tradition, Ōgetsuhime came on horseback from Niu-no-gō in {{ill|lt=Ise Province|Q864166|WD=Q864166|1=Ise Province}} (present {{ill|lt=Niu (Taki, Mie)|Q17213591|WD=Q17213591|lt=Niu|1=Nyū}}, {{ill|lt=Taki|Q1203963|WD=Q1203963|1=Taki, Mie}}, {{ill|lt=Taki District|Q1042360|WD=Q1042360|1=Taki District, Mie}}, {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196|1=Mie Prefecture}}) to Awa Province and spread millet in this area. === History === It is one candidate shrine for the {{ill|lt=major Shikinaisha|Q134917288|WD=Q134917288|1=Shikinai Taisha}} "Awa Province, Nakata District, Ame-no-Iwato-wake Yakura-hime Shrine" listed in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'. In {{ill|lt=Jinki|Q1373210|WD=Q1373210|1=Jinki (era)}} 5 ({{ill|lt=728|Q23560|WD=Q23560|1=728}}), Ame-no-Iwato-wake Yakura-hime Shrine became an {{ill|lt=imperial prayer site|Q22131160|WD=Q22131160|1=Chokugansho}} of {{ill|lt=Emperor Shōmu|Q349263|WD=Q349263|1=Emperor Shōmu}}, and in {{ill|lt=Genryaku|Q1194250|WD=Q1194250|1=Genryaku}} 2 ({{ill|lt=1185|Q19729|WD=Q19729|1=1185}}) received Junior First Rank in {{ill|lt=shinkai|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}}. In the Heian period, {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya Shrine (Tokushima)|Q11352598|WD=Q11352598|lt=Ichinomiya Shrine|1=Ichinomiya Shrine (Tokushima)}} was founded in present {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya-chō (Tokushima)|Q11352592|WD=Q11352592|lt=Ichinomiya-chō|1=Ichinomiya-chō}}, {{ill|lt=Tokushima|Q207309|WD=Q207309|1=Tokushima (city)}}, as a branch of this shrine. In Meiji 3 ({{ill|lt=1870|Q7741|WD=Q7741|1=1870}}), the shrine name was changed to "Haniu-meya Shrine," but by petition of parishioners it became the current Kamiichinomiya Ōawa Shrine in Meiji 28 ({{ill|lt=1895|Q7844|WD=Q7844|1=1895}}). === Precinct === * Great torii * Shinmon gate: with sacred statues on both sides; from here the first half of the approach is earth path and the second half steep stone steps to the precinct * Main shrine haiden and honden * Sessha: Yasaka Shrine * Sessha: small shrine to the right of shrine office * Sessha: Akiba Shrine * Ōawayama Jingū-ji: Shikoku 33 Kannon site No.4, Kōyasan Shingon, principal image Monju Bosatsu, founded in 729 by Gyōki as this shrine's bettō temple by imperial order of Emperor Shōmu Past the large white torii is the shinmon gate, and stone steps from there lead up to the precinct. Taking the road right of the great torii, at the final fork left leads to this shrine and right to Jingū-ji. Up to microbus size can pass. There is broad parking around the main shrine. It is temple No.73 of the {{ill|lt=New Shikoku Mandala Sacred Sites|Q11501519|WD=Q11501519|1=Shin-Shikoku Mandara Pilgrimage}}. === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|lt=Ōgetsuhime|Q3261482|WD=Q3261482|lt=Ōgetsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=Ōgetsuhime}} (おおげつひめのみこと) - also called Ame-no-Iwato-wake Yakura-hime-no-Mikoto or Ōawa-hime-no-Mikoto. Honden and haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734|WD=Q160734|1=Tokushima Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Myōzai District|Q1155732|WD=Q1155732|1=Myōzai District, Tokushima}}{{ill|lt=Kamiyama|Q1350922|WD=Q1350922|1=Kamiyama, Tokushima}}Jinryō Aza Nishi-Uwagaku 330 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|58|15.6|N|134|22|1|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Ōgetsuhime|Q3261482|WD=Q3261482|lt=Ōgetsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=Ōgetsuhime}} |社格 = {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha major shrine|Q134917288|WD=Q134917288|1=Shikinai Taisha}} {{ill|lt=candidate shrine|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}
{{ill|lt=Awa Province|Q907457|WD=Q907457|1=Awa Province (Tokushima)}} {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|1=Ichinomiya}} candidate shrine
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = No.73 of {{ill|lt=New Shikoku Mandala Sacred Sites|Q11501519|WD=Q11501519|1=Shin-Shikoku Mandara Pilgrimage}} |例祭 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sports Day|1=UNKNOWN}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Tokushima|label=Kamiichinomiya Ōawa Shrine|position=left|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Kamiichinomiya Ōawa Shrine''' (かみいちのみやおおあわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} on {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Mt. Ōawa|1=UNKNOWN}} in {{ill|lt=Kamiyama|Q1350922|WD=Q1350922|1=Kamiyama, Tokushima}}, {{ill|lt=Myōzai District|Q1155732|WD=Q1155732|1=Myōzai District, Tokushima}}, {{ill|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734|WD=Q160734|1=Tokushima Prefecture}}. It is one candidate shrine for "Ame-no-Iwato-wake Yakura-hime Shrine," a {{ill|lt=major Shikinaisha|Q134917288|WD=Q134917288|1=Shikinai Taisha}} and ichinomiya of {{ill|lt=Awa Province|Q907457|WD=Q907457|1=Awa Province (Tokushima)}}Other candidate shrines are {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yakura-hime Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} and {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya Shrine (Tokushima)|Q11352598|WD=Q11352598|lt=Ichinomiya Shrine|1=Ichinomiya Shrine (Tokushima)}}.. Its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. It is temple No.73 of the {{ill|lt=New Shikoku Mandala Sacred Sites|Q11501519|WD=Q11501519|1=Shin-Shikoku Mandara Pilgrimage}}. === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|lt=Ōgetsuhime|Q3261482|WD=Q3261482|lt=Ōgetsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=Ōgetsuhime}} (おおげつひめのみこと) - also called Ame-no-Iwato-wake Yakura-hime-no-Mikoto or Ōawa-hime-no-Mikoto. According to shrine tradition, Ōgetsuhime came on horseback from Niu-no-gō in {{ill|lt=Ise Province|Q864166|WD=Q864166|1=Ise Province}} (present {{ill|lt=Niu (Taki, Mie)|Q17213591|WD=Q17213591|lt=Niu|1=Nyū}}, {{ill|lt=Taki|Q1203963|WD=Q1203963|1=Taki, Mie}}, {{ill|lt=Taki District|Q1042360|WD=Q1042360|1=Taki District, Mie}}, {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196|1=Mie Prefecture}}) to Awa Province and spread millet in this area. === History === It is one candidate shrine for the {{ill|lt=major Shikinaisha|Q134917288|WD=Q134917288|1=Shikinai Taisha}} "Awa Province, Nakata District, Ame-no-Iwato-wake Yakura-hime Shrine" listed in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'. In {{ill|lt=Jinki|Q1373210|WD=Q1373210|1=Jinki (era)}} 5 ({{ill|lt=728|Q23560|WD=Q23560|1=728}}), Ame-no-Iwato-wake Yakura-hime Shrine became an {{ill|lt=imperial prayer site|Q22131160|WD=Q22131160|1=Chokugansho}} of {{ill|lt=Emperor Shōmu|Q349263|WD=Q349263|1=Emperor Shōmu}}, and in {{ill|lt=Genryaku|Q1194250|WD=Q1194250|1=Genryaku}} 2 ({{ill|lt=1185|Q19729|WD=Q19729|1=1185}}) received Junior First Rank in {{ill|lt=shinkai|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}}. In the Heian period, {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya Shrine (Tokushima)|Q11352598|WD=Q11352598|lt=Ichinomiya Shrine|1=Ichinomiya Shrine (Tokushima)}} was founded in present {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya-chō (Tokushima)|Q11352592|WD=Q11352592|lt=Ichinomiya-chō|1=Ichinomiya-chō}}, {{ill|lt=Tokushima|Q207309|WD=Q207309|1=Tokushima (city)}}, as a branch of this shrine. In Meiji 3 ({{ill|lt=1870|Q7741|WD=Q7741|1=1870}}), the shrine name was changed to "Haniu-meya Shrine," but by petition of parishioners it became the current Kamiichinomiya Ōawa Shrine in Meiji 28 ({{ill|lt=1895|Q7844|WD=Q7844|1=1895}}). === Precinct === * Great torii * Shinmon gate: with sacred statues on both sides; from here the first half of the approach is earth path and the second half steep stone steps to the precinct * Main shrine haiden and honden * Sessha: Yasaka Shrine * Sessha: small shrine to the right of shrine office * Sessha: Akiba Shrine * Ōawayama Jingū-ji: Shikoku 33 Kannon site No.4, Kōyasan Shingon, principal image Monju Bosatsu, founded in 729 by Gyōki as this shrine's bettō temple by imperial order of Emperor Shōmu Past the large white torii is the shinmon gate, and stone steps from there lead up to the precinct. Taking the road right of the great torii, at the final fork left leads to this shrine and right to Jingū-ji. Up to microbus size can pass. There is broad parking around the main shrine. It is temple No.73 of the {{ill|lt=New Shikoku Mandala Sacred Sites|Q11501519|qid=Q11501519|1=Shin-Shikoku Mandara Pilgrimage}}. === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|lt=Ōgetsuhime|Q3261482|qid=Q3261482|lt=Ōgetsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=Ōgetsuhime}} (おおげつひめのみこと) - also called Ame-no-Iwato-wake Yakura-hime-no-Mikoto or Ōawa-hime-no-Mikoto. According to shrine tradition, Ōgetsuhime came on horseback from Niu-no-gō in {{ill|lt=Ise Province|Q864166|qid=Q864166|1=Ise Province}} (present {{ill|lt=Niu (Taki, Mie)|Q17213591|qid=Q17213591|lt=Niu|1=Nyū}}, {{ill|lt=Taki|Q1203963|qid=Q1203963|1=Taki, Mie}}, {{ill|lt=Taki District|Q1042360|qid=Q1042360|1=Taki District, Mie}}, {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|qid=Q128196|1=Mie Prefecture}}) to Awa Province and spread millet in this area. === History === It is one candidate shrine for the {{ill|lt=major Shikinaisha|Q134917288|qid=Q134917288|1=Shikinai Taisha}} "Awa Province, Nakata District, Ame-no-Iwato-wake Yakura-hime Shrine" listed in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'. In {{ill|lt=Jinki|Q1373210|qid=Q1373210|1=Jinki (era)}} 5 ({{ill|lt=728|Q23560|qid=Q23560|1=728}}), Ame-no-Iwato-wake Yakura-hime Shrine became an {{ill|lt=imperial prayer site|Q22131160|qid=Q22131160|1=Chokugansho}} of {{ill|lt=Emperor Shōmu|Q349263|qid=Q349263|1=Emperor Shōmu}}, and in {{ill|lt=Genryaku|Q1194250|qid=Q1194250|1=Genryaku}} 2 ({{ill|lt=1185|Q19729|qid=Q19729|1=1185}}) received Junior First Rank in {{ill|lt=shinkai|Q11591025|qid=Q11591025|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}}. In the Heian period, {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya Shrine (Tokushima)|Q11352598|qid=Q11352598|lt=Ichinomiya Shrine|1=Ichinomiya Shrine (Tokushima)}} was founded in present {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya-chō (Tokushima)|Q11352592|qid=Q11352592|lt=Ichinomiya-chō|1=Ichinomiya-chō}}, {{ill|lt=Tokushima|Q207309|qid=Q207309|1=Tokushima (city)}}, as a branch of this shrine. In Meiji 3 ({{ill|lt=1870|Q7741|qid=Q7741|1=1870}}), the shrine name was changed to "Haniu-meya Shrine," but by petition of parishioners it became the current Kamiichinomiya Ōawa Shrine in Meiji 28 ({{ill|lt=1895|Q7844|qid=Q7844|1=1895}}). === Precinct === * Great torii * Shinmon gate: with sacred statues on both sides; from here the first half of the approach is earth path and the second half steep stone steps to the precinct * Main shrine haiden and honden * Sessha: Yasaka Shrine * Sessha: small shrine to the right of shrine office * Sessha: Akiba Shrine * Ōawayama Jingū-ji: Shikoku 33 Kannon site No.4, Kōyasan Shingon, principal image Monju Bosatsu, founded in 729 by Gyōki as this shrine's bettō temple by imperial order of Emperor Shōmu Past the large white torii is the shinmon gate, and stone steps from there lead up to the precinct. Taking the road right of the great torii, at the final fork left leads to this shrine and right to Jingū-ji. Up to microbus size can pass. There is broad parking around the main shrine. Ooawa shrine 20250918 1.jpg|Great torii Ooawa shirine 20250918 2.jpg|Shinmon gate Ooawa shrine 20250918 6.jpg|Main shrine haiden Ooawa shirine 20250918 3.jpg|Sessha Yasaka Shrine === Previous and next sacred sites === ;{{ill|lt=New Shikoku Mandala Sacred Sites|Q11501519|qid=Q11501519|1=Shin-Shikoku Mandara Pilgrimage}} : 72 {{ill|lt=Myōhō-ji (Kamiyama, Tokushima)|Q65260214|qid=Q65260214|lt=Myōhō-ji|1=Myouhouji Temple}} --- '''73 Kamiichinomiya Ōawa Shrine''' --- 74 {{ill|lt=Hōzō-ji (Sanagōchi, Tokushima)|Q65261267|qid=Q65261267|lt=Hōzō-ji|1=Hōzō-ji Temple (Sanagōchi, Tokushima)}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === See also === {{Commons|Category:Kamiichinomiya_Oawa-jinja}} '''Shrines claiming Awa ichinomiya''' * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yakura-hime Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya Shrine (Tokushima)|Q11352598|qid=Q11352598|1=Ichinomiya Shrine (Tokushima)}} * {{ill|lt=Ōasahiko Shrine|Q723417|qid=Q723417|1=Ōasahiko Shrine}} {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kamiichinomiya Ōawa Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kamiichinomiya Ōawa Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kamiichinomiya Ōawa Shrine]][[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:神山町の建築物]] [[Category:神山町の歴史]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:New Shikoku Mandala Sacred Sites]] [[Category:Awa Province (Tokushima)]]
